# For Integration Developers

# Prebuilt Integration Creation Guide

This document outlines the process that needs to be followed when developing a prebuilt integration.Tool Stack:

Tool Stack

* VSCode BI Extension(Devant Cloud Editor)  
* Low-Code Environment  
* AI Copilot

Environment Guidelines

* Cloud-First Policy: All integrations must begin in the Cloud Editor with AI assistance.  
* The "Stop" Rule: Switching to Local/Pro-Code is only permitted after an Issue Ticket is opened and approved.

### Step 1: Develop the integration

- Once the scenario is finalized, decide on the configurables that we are going to expose. For example, for an integration that exports all Salesforce Opportunities to a google sheet, possible configurables would be,  
  - Timeframe  
  - Whether to use an existing spreadsheet or create a new one  
- Use the WSO2 Integrator: BI to develop the integration on the WSO2 Integration Platform Cloud Editor. Take advantage of the BI Copilot with the low-code mode.  
- Always use Ballerina Connectors available in [central.ballerina.io](https://central.ballerina.io).  
- If you encounter problems when trying to develop using either the copilot or low-code mode, please create an issue in [wso2/product-integrator](https://github.com/wso2/product-integrator). You may switch to pro-code mode in those cases, after consulting the WSO2 Integration Platform (SaaS) team.   
- Try to use the low-code mode as much as possible to detect usability issues. It will be beneficial to create a separate doc outlining the issues encountered.  
- When using the pro-code mode, make sure you follow the [Ballerina Coding Conventions](https://ballerina.io/learn/style-guide/coding-conventions/).  
- Once development is complete, thoroughly test the integration and ensure that edge cases are handled gracefully.  
- In the \`Ballerina.toml\`, ensure the following are set.

```
[package]
org = "wso2"
name = "new_shopify_customer_to_stripe"
version = "0.1.0"
title = "new-shopify-customer-to-stripe"
distribution= "2201.13.1"
```

- Ensure the \`org\` is set to \`wso2\`  
- \`name\` must clearly represent the purpose of the integration, without being lengthy. This name must be identical to the directory name, except the delimiter should be underscore (\_) instead of the dash (-). This is **CRITICAL**, or else the build process fails.  
- Set the \`version\` to \`0.1.0\`  
- For the \`title\`, it is okay to use dashed delimited version of the \`name\`  
- Ensure the \`distribution\` is set to the latest available version

### Step 2: Build the integration

Use the below command to build an Automation type integration. This will generate the \`config-schema.json\` file which will be needed in Step 3\.

```
bal build --generate-config-schema --cloud docker
```

Use the below command to build an Integration as API type Integration. This will generate the \`config-schema.json\` file and the \`openapi.yaml\` file which contains the OpenAPI specification, which will be needed in Step 3\.

```
bal build --generate-config-schema --export-openapi --cloud docker
```

Please note that the \`--cloud docker\` flag is used to ensure that the build completes smoothly without dependency conflicts when building the image in the GitHub Workflow. Artifacts generated from it are not needed.

### Step 3: Document the integration

- To deliver the best experience of a prebuilt integration, proper documentation is required. Please make sure the following documentation is done before making the pull request.  
- Create the \`README.md\` file at the root of the directory. The structure of the file should be similar to [this example](https://github.com/wso2/integration-samples/blob/main/ballerina-integrator/salesforceops-to-googlesheets/README.md).  
- Create a \`.choreo\` directory at the root of the integration, and add the following files.

- .choreo/diagram.md  
  - This markdown file contains the flow chart defined in mermaid syntax that is rendered when selecting the prebuilt integration in the WSO2 Integration Platform.

  	![][image1]

  - This needs to use the below node styles appropriately.  
    - startNode: Denotes a start node

      ![][image2]

    - endNode: Denotes an end node

      ![][image3]

    - processNode: Denotes a process node

      ![][image4]

    - decisionNode: Denotes a decision node

      ![][image5]

  - Refer to this [example](https://github.com/wso2/integration-samples/blob/main/ballerina-integrator/salesforceops-to-googlesheets/.choreo/diagram.md).  
- .choreo/instructions.md  
  - This markdown file is rendered during prebuilt integration setup in the WSO2 Integration Platform.  
  - Refer to this [example](https://github.com/wso2/integration-samples/blob/main/ballerina-integrator/salesforceops-to-googlesheets/.choreo/instructions.md). Ensure the structure is similar.  
- .choreo/config-schema.json  
  - This JSON file is used to render the configuration form during prebuilt integration setup in the WSO2 Integration Platform.  
  - This has to be generated with the \`bal build\` command, which was outlined in Step 2\.  
  - In the generated JSON object, please add the following key-value pair to the end of the JSON object.

```json
"requiredLevel": 3
```

![][image6]

- .choreo/component.yaml  
  - This is required only for Integration as APIs and AI agents.  
  - Refer to this [example](https://github.com/wso2/integration-samples/blob/main/ballerina-integrator/signup-sfchatsync/.choreo/component.yaml).  
- .choreo/openapi.yaml  
  - This is the generated openapi specification with the \`bal build\` command, as outlined in Step 2\.

### Step 4: Delete unnecessary artifacts

- Ensure that the \`target\` directory is not pushed, as well as the \`Dependencies.toml\` file. Also ensure that the \`target\` directory is properly ignored in the \`.gitignore\`.

### Step 5: Make the Pull Request

The pull request must be made to the [wso2/integration-samples](https://github.com/wso2/integration-samples) repository. Prebuilt integrations live inside the \`ballerina-integrator\` directory.

# PR Reviewers

# PR Review Checklist

In addition to the general review process, please ensure the below. Integration developers can also use this as a checklist before submitting the PR.

- [ ] .github/workflows/projects.json is updated with the new prebuilt integration path  
- [ ] Path in the .github/workflows/projects.json matches the project path exactly  
- [ ] Directory path is identical to the integration name in \`Ballerina.toml\`  
- [ ] README.MD is correctly structured  
- [ ] .choreo/config-schema.json exists and is up to date  
- [ ] .choreo/choreo-config-schema.json exists and is up to date  
- [ ] .choreo/instructions.md is correctly structured  
- [ ] .choreo/diagram.md is correctly written and parsable by Mermaid  
- [ ] .choreo/component.yaml exists for service type integrations  
- [ ] .choreo/openapi.yaml exists for service type integrations
